gene_description: ATP-binding cassette sub-family G member 2 — encodes BCRP (Breast Cancer Resistance Protein), an efflux transporter in intestine, liver, kidney, and blood-brain barrier that controls bioavailability of statins, urate-lowering drugs, and many oncology agents

source.title: The Association between ABCG2 421C>A (rs2231142) Polymorphism and Rosuvastatin Pharmacokinetics: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
source.finding: ABCG2 Q141K (rs2231142) significantly increases rosuvastatin AUC and Cmax in a gene-dose dependent manner. Homozygous TT carriers show ~2.4-fold higher rosuvastatin exposure. CPIC recommends initial dose ≤20mg for homozygous Q141K carriers.

TT.effect: Homozygous Q141K carriers have markedly reduced BCRP efflux activity. Rosuvastatin AUC increased ~2.4-fold, with significantly higher Cmax. Increased risk of statin-associated musculoskeletal symptoms (SAMS). For allopurinol, DPWG recommends 1.4-fold higher dose due to association with decreased efficacy. Also affects sulfasalazine bioavailability.
TT.recommendation: Rosuvastatin: use initial dose ≤20mg per CPIC guideline. Consider alternative statin (pravastatin, fluvastatin) if myopathy occurs. Allopurinol: consider 1.4x higher dose per DPWG. Monitor uric acid levels closely. Monitor for statin side effects including myalgia.

GT.effect: Heterozygotes show intermediate BCRP activity. Rosuvastatin AUC increased ~1.7-fold. Modest increase in statin exposure with slightly elevated risk of myopathy. For allopurinol, DPWG recommends 1.25-fold higher dose.
GT.recommendation: Rosuvastatin: standard dosing acceptable but monitor for myopathy. Consider dose cap at 20mg if adverse effects emerge. Allopurinol: consider 1.25x dose adjustment per DPWG if target uric acid not reached.

TG.effect: Heterozygotes show intermediate BCRP activity. Rosuvastatin AUC increased ~1.7-fold. Modest increase in statin exposure with slightly elevated risk of myopathy. For allopurinol, DPWG recommends 1.25-fold higher dose.
TG.recommendation: Rosuvastatin: standard dosing acceptable but monitor for myopathy. Consider dose cap at 20mg if adverse effects emerge. Allopurinol: consider 1.25x dose adjustment per DPWG if target uric acid not reached.

GG.effect: Wild-type with full BCRP efflux transporter activity. Normal rosuvastatin pharmacokinetics and standard allopurinol response. Normal intestinal and hepatic drug efflux.
GG.recommendation: Standard dosing for rosuvastatin and allopurinol. No BCRP-related dose adjustments needed.
